message:
feat(error_renderer): route typed PaneNotFound through FT-1010 + pin with golden test

Preserve the operator-facing pane-not-found contract that existed before
the typed runtime/pane/watchdog refactor in 79702a50 ("ux(errors): type
runtime, pane, and watchdog failures"). Previously the typed
Error::PaneOperation { source: PaneNotFound, .. } variant fell through
to the generic FT-9001 "internal" bucket (via the [ft-h9g0q] fallback),
which degraded the diagnostic from a specific "pane N not found" code
into an opaque "internal error" for the exact failure mode operators
encounter most often.

## Routing fix

crates/frankenterm-core/src/output/error_renderer.rs

Add a specific match arm BEFORE the generic PaneOperation catch-all:

  Error::PaneOperation {
      source: PaneOperationSource::PaneNotFound,
      ..
  } => "FT-1010",

This preserves the existing FT-1010 ("pane not found") contract that
the pre-typed Error::Wezterm(WeztermError::PaneNotFound { .. }) path
mapped to. Other typed PaneOperation sources (non-PaneNotFound)
continue to fall through to FT-9001 with the [ft-h9g0q] fallback
comment, unchanged. Import `PaneOperationSource` from crate::error.

The [ft-h9g0q] comment block is compressed — the new in-file comment
explicitly states the remaining variants "still share the generic
internal bucket until dedicated catalog codes are assigned in
error_codes.rs", which is the honest status.

## Golden test pins the contract

New test `typed_pane_not_found_renderer_matches_golden` exercises the
full JSON + plain renderer output for the canonical fixture
  Error::PaneOperation {
      pane_id: 42,
      operation: "inject",
      source: PaneOperationSource::PaneNotFound,
  }
and compares against a BTreeMap-sorted, 2-space pretty-printed golden
file at:
  crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/error_renderer_typed_pane_not_found.json

Output shape captures BOTH renderer surfaces in one fixture:
  {
    "plain_lines": ["...", "..."],  // plain render split by newlines
    "json": { ... }                  // full structured JSON render
  }

Regenerate with:
  UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib \
    typed_pane_not_found_renderer_matches_golden

read_or_update_golden + assert_matches_golden helpers (also new) write
a .actual.json sibling on drift so an operator can diff before
re-baselining.

## Cheap contract pin

New test `typed_pane_not_found_uses_pane_not_found_code` is a one-line
pin: it just asserts ErrorRenderer::error_code(&error) == "FT-1010"
for the canonical fixture. Fast, catches the routing regression
independently of golden drift.

## Test-module imports

Add `serde_json::{Map, Value, json}` and `std::path::PathBuf` to the
tests module `use` block so the canonicalize / pretty_canonical /
golden helpers can reference them without crossing module boundaries.

## Beads

Transition ft-fvt0b (codec oversized-container regression overconstrains
error wording) from `open` to `in_progress` with assignee=pane8 — that
bead's fix already landed in c93fa5f7 (the loosened stream_decode
rejection assertion) but the bead itself wasn't reflecting the
in-flight status until this commit. Left in_progress pending closure
verification on the next sweep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","title":"[ft-e34d9.10.5.2][support] Strengthen cancellation-under-contention invariants in mux migration completion harness","description":"Bounded test-only support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is limited to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Strengthen the currently shallow cancellation coverage by adding a contention-focused invariant: a cancelled acquire while the only pool permit is held must fail cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious downstream mux activity, and the subsequent healthy release/retry path must restore a balanced pool. Validate with rch-only targeted cargo evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyRaven","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441229Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancellation","integration-tests","test-fix","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2605,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Started bounded support slice on crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Plan: strengthen cancellation-under-contention coverage so a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held fails cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious mux activity, then prove the subsequent healthy release/retry path restores balance. File reservation is active via Agent Mail #6191; validation will be rch-only targeted cargo.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:57Z"},{"id":2607,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Patch landed in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Added a new cancellation-under-contention regression that mirrors the existing timeout-cascade recovery shape but uses user cancellation and stronger invariants: blocked acquire must surface cancellation, preserve zero available permits while the primary checkout is held, avoid incrementing total_acquired, and avoid issuing extra mock mux requests before a healthy release/retry restores balance. Local rustfmt and git diff --check are green. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on worker vmi1153651: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:17:11Z"},{"id":2611,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Remote validation completed cleanly on worker vmi1153651. Command: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1. Result: 1 passed / 0 failed. This confirms the new regression in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs correctly proves a cancelled nested acquire under contention does not leak permits, does not count as a successful checkout, does not issue an extra mux request, and still allows the following healthy release/retry path to restore a balanced pool.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:30:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e8brh","title":"[MEDIUM] cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race coverage; passes trivially via propagation","description":"In commit 6a16ae0a (fix(ft-2xnop): add unix umask coverage ... +
cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626237Z","close_reason":"Hardened in 7ac677a6: creator count 8→64 (forces preemption on any host), per-thread yield_now + modulo 50µs sleep widens race, rounds 20→64, each creator samples is_cancelled() pre-child() and the round-level outcomes are checked so the test asserts the cancel-first ordering was actually exercised at least once — if it isn't, the test fails with a diagnostic pointing at loom/shuttle for deterministic coverage. Build clean; local run blocked by unrelated macOS dyld bottleneck under load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-fbee5","title":"pattern pack discovery followed symlinks out of tree","description":"# [HIGH] pattern-pack loader: symlink-escape via discover_packs_from_dir

**Subsystem:** patterns / scan_pipeline
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs

## The hole

`load_pack_from_file(path, root)` routes through `sandbox_resolve` only when a
`Some(root)` is supplied. `discover_packs_from_dir`, used for
`.ft/patterns/**` discovery from a workspace root, was calling
`load_pack_from_file(path, None)` for every file + rules.toml it iterated —
so the sandbox check was **skipped** even though the dir itself was
workspace-attacker-controlled.

A hostile repo could ship:

    .ft/patterns/config.yaml -> /Users/victim/.ssh/id_rsa

`path.is_file()` follows the symlink (returns true), `load_pack_from_file`
opens the target, feeds its contents to `serde_yaml::from_str`, and on parse
failure emits:

    tracing::warn!("Skipping invalid user pack {}: {e}", path.display());

Which can echo excerpts of the target file via serde's error reporting
(`mapping expected, found: …`).

## Pre-conditions

* victim clones a hostile repo that contains a symlink under
  `.ft/patterns/`;
* frankenterm loads user packs at session start.

## Fix

`discover_packs_from_dir` now passes `Some(dir)` as the sandbox root to
`load_pack_from_file`. `sandbox_resolve` canonicalizes and rejects any
resolved path outside `dir`. Added regression test
`discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape`.

## Residual risk

A top-level symlink replacing `.ft/patterns` itself would still escape
because the caller (`load_packs_from_config`) builds `ws_dir =
root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns")` without canonicalizing. That's a
follow-up — tracking separately.

## Sibling work

- 33e84436 security(patterns): add sandbox_resolve helper (helper introduction)
- e8397747 / 8e57c8e0 fix(limits): MAX_PACK_BYTES 16 MiB cap (orthogonal DoS layer)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:10.019601Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.244142Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.243799Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 6ab52765 — canonicalize + in-tree check applied to pack discovery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ft-05hfm","patterns","security"]}

 {"id":"ft-gofz5","title":"[EPIC] Release engineering: first tagged release, cargo install validation, and binary distribution pipeline","description":"# Goal
Establish a working release pipeline so that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` actually works, release binaries are available for major platforms, and there are tagged versions users can reference.

# Background
The reality check found:
- Only 1 git tag exists ("backup-before-rewrite") — no version releases
- \`cargo install\` is advertised in README Quick Install but never validated
- 6 GitHub Actions workflows exist (ci.yml, release.yml, etc.) but release.yml has never been triggered for a real release
- The project is pre-1.0 with 4,288 commits but zero releases
- Build is resource-intensive (~793k LOC core library) which may cause cargo install to fail on memory-constrained machines

# Why This Matters
A project that can't be installed by its advertised method has zero users. Even for internal/swarm use, tagged releases let agents pin to known-good versions. Release hygiene signals project maturity.

# Architectural Intent
1. Validate that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` succeeds on a clean machine (Linux x86_64, macOS arm64 at minimum).
2. Create v0.1.0 tag with honest release notes reflecting current capabilities.
3. Ensure release.yml workflow produces downloadable binaries.
4. Add a CI job that runs \`cargo install\` from git to catch install regressions.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. \`cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft\` succeeds on Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
2. A v0.1.0 tag exists with release notes that honestly describe working features, partial features, and known limitations.
3. release.yml produces downloadable binaries for at least Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
4. CI includes a cargo install smoke test that runs on every push to main.
5. README Quick Install section includes minimum system requirements (RAM, disk, Rust nightly version).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:35.783442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344593Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","distribution","goal-line","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:59:00.127254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.1","title":"Validate cargo install from git on clean Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64 environments","description":"# Goal
Prove that the advertised Quick Install command actually works on fresh machines.

# Design
1. On a clean Linux x86_64 environment (CI runner or fresh container):
   - Install Rust nightly (matching rust-toolchain.toml)
   - Run: cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft
   - Verify: ft --version produces output
   - Verify: ft doctor runs without crash
   - Record: peak RAM usage, disk usage, wall time

2. On macOS arm64 (CI runner or local):
   - Same steps as above
   - Additional: verify LLVM/clang dependency resolution (cc alias issue documented in MEMORY.md)

3. If install fails, diagnose and fix:
   - Likely issues: memory exhaustion (793k LOC), missing system deps, cc alias conflict
   - Fix in Cargo.toml, build.rs, or CI configuration
   - Document minimum requirements

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo install succeeds on both platforms with exit code 0.
2. ft --version and ft doctor both work post-install.
3. Peak RAM and disk requirements documented.
4. If build requires CC/CXX overrides, document in README.","notes":"2026-04-07 CyanIvy: install/docs command corrected; targeted docs smoke test passes via rch; clean-host harness now passes and records WezTerm bridge prerequisites honestly; remaining gap is true clean-host cargo-install-from-git validation on Linux/macOS because rch in this environment is classifying cargo install as non-offloaded. Added workflow .github/workflows/cargo-install-validation.yml for that next step.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.274240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.261176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","install","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.2","title":"Create v0.1.0 tagged release with honest release notes and downloadable binaries","description":"# Goal
Cut the first real version release with honest release notes.

# Design
1. Version bump: Set version = "0.1.0" in relevant Cargo.toml files
2. Write release notes that honestly categorize:
   - Working: observation pipeline, pattern detection, Robot Mode, FTS search, policy engine, fleet memory, tiered scrollback, BOCPD, session persistence, connector SDK
   - Feature-gated: MCP, distributed mode, semantic search, web API, TUI
   - Partial: tx execution (orchestration framework complete, data plane in progress), mission dispatch
   - Known limitations: resource-intensive build, Rust nightly required, WezTerm backend dependency
3. Trigger release.yml workflow
4. Verify downloadable binaries are produced for Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Git tag v0.1.0 exists with annotated release notes.
2. GitHub Release page has downloadable binaries.
3. Release notes are honest — no feature is listed as working unless it passes end-to-end.
4. CHANGELOG.md updated with v0.1.0 section.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.025577Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.022546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.706256Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
